operating_state: Total consolidated revenues increased to $4.3 billion for the six months ended June 30, 2025, driven by rent growth from lease rollovers capturing accumulated market rent increases and operating distributions from unconsolidated entities rising to $292 million from $256 million. Rent change on new and renewed leases reached 53.6% on a net effective basis, reflecting the 22% lease mark-to-market opportunity across the portfolio. Occupancy remained strong at 95.0% with leasing activity accelerating through the second quarter despite tariff policy uncertainty since April.
cost_structure: Real Estate Segment NOI increased $280 million to $3.0 billion, primarily from higher rental rates on lease rollovers and development completions. Interest payments net of capitalized amounts rose to $513 million from $408 million, while rental expenses increased modestly from higher property taxes and operating costs on the expanded portfolio. General and administrative expenses increased marginally while equity-based noncash compensation expenses decreased.
material_events: The company completed $3.05 billion in debt financing including $1.8 billion of senior notes with 4.8% weighted average interest rate and established a new $3.0 billion global credit facility extending maturity to June 2029. Development starts totaled $1.5 billion with 69.6% build-to-suit projects, while property dispositions generated $270 million in net proceeds. Joseph Ghazal stepped down as Chief Investment Officer effective July 1, 2025.
macro_exposures: Tariff policy uncertainty since April has created economic headwinds, though customer dialogue indicates continued forward momentum in supply chain decision-making. Foreign currency exposure remains significant across international operations with natural hedging through local currency borrowings partially mitigating translation risk. Unconsolidated co-investment ventures maintained $18.6 billion in third-party debt with 29.2% weighted average loan-to-value ratio.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued organic NOI growth driven by the substantial 22% lease mark-to-market opportunity as in-place leases roll to higher market rents. The development pipeline includes $5.2 billion TEI across 84 properties that are 36.5% leased, requiring $2.3 billion additional investment through completion before September 2027. Quarterly common stock dividends increased to $1.01 per share in 2025 from $0.96 in 2024.
uncertainty_profile: The company maintains outstanding equity commitments to co-investment ventures through 2033 and faces debt maturities requiring refinancing or repayment. REIT status compliance requires meeting specific federal tax obligations, while preferred stock dividend restrictions prevent common stock dividends unless all cumulative preferred dividends are paid.
